claim: Multi-agent orchestration is the next level after custom agents; one orchestrator agent manages, creates, and monitors multiple specialized agents
actionable: Build an orchestrator agent that spins up and coordinates task-focused agents instead of using a single monolithic agent
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Observability is critical for multi-agent systems; if you can't measure agent performance and costs, you can't scale
actionable: Build real-time observability dashboards showing all agent responses, tool calls, thinking traces, and costs per agent
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: CRUD operations for agents enable true agent scaling; agents are temporary resources created, used, and deleted by the orchestrator
actionable: Design agent lifecycle management: create agents per task, run them, monitor results, delete when done; don't reuse persistent agents
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: The core four of agent management: context, model, prompt, tools; these must be tuned for each agent task
actionable: Before spinning up an agent, lock in its context window size, model choice, system prompt, and available tools
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Protect orchestrator context by not always reading all agent logs; use dedicated status-check agents instead
actionable: When orchestrator needs summary of agent work, have a separate agent read logs and summarize rather than flooding orchestrator context
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Multi-file edits in a single prompt are a significant advantage; agents can refactor across your entire codebase at once
actionable: Use AI tools that support multi-file editing (like Aider) to refactor or update multiple files in one agent interaction
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Use sleeping patterns for agents; let one agent start work, orchestrator checks status every N seconds, then moves to next task
actionable: Implement agent workflow loops with polling intervals; orchestrator can manage multiple concurrent agent tasks
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Every agent must produce concrete results (files, summaries, output); if no concrete artifact, the agent added no value
actionable: Design agent prompts to always output specific files or artifacts; avoid pure analysis without tangible results
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Specialized tools built for your domain beat generic cloud-based tools; customize all the way down for maximum effectiveness
actionable: Build domain-specific agent orchestration tools rather than relying on out-of-the-box AI coding platforms
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Out-of-loop orchestration systems are more powerful than in-loop tools; deploy agents without human in terminal loop
actionable: Build systems where agents complete work independently with observability; reduce back-and-forth prompting
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Human-in-the-loop decision points are critical; agents should ask humans questions rather than assume decisions
actionable: Add agent decision points that prompt for human confirmation on risky or ambiguous choices
source_title: The One Agent to RULE them ALL - Advanced Agentic Coding
